chunk_summary: This section discusses donor eligibility screening and periodic reassessment for milk banking, emphasizing safety, guidelines, and best practices for donor human milk usage.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for donor eligibility in human milk banking.
Learn the importance of periodic reassessment for milk donors.
Familiarize with national and international guidelines on donor milk safety and usage.
Explore best practices for managing donor milk for high-risk infants.
key_facts: Donor eligibility screening ensures milk safety and includes health history, lifestyle, and infectious disease testing.
Periodic reassessment of donors is critical to maintain milk quality and safety standards.
National guidelines, such as those from India, provide frameworks for lactation management centers.
The American Academy of Pediatrics emphasizes donor milk safety for high-risk infants in the United States.
Best practices for human milk banking include pasteurization and rigorous quality control measures.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Donor human milk for the high-risk infant: preparation, safety, and usage options in the United States — https://publications.aap.org/pediatrics/article/139/1/e20163440/52000/DonorHuman-Milk-for-the-High-Risk-Infant
document_key_topics: Safety and quality control in milk banking
